paper_summary.main_contribution: The paper demonstrates the successful biosynthesis of selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) using fenugreek seeds extract, highlighting their antifungal properties, low cytotoxicity, and potential to enhance the shelf-life and physico-chemical properties of tomato fruits.
paper_summary.narrative: The paper begins by confirming the successful biosynthesis of SeNPs through XRD and UV-VIS spectroscopy (Fig. 1), showing characteristic peaks and absorbance. The elemental composition and size distribution of SeNPs are further confirmed by EDX, TEM, and FTIR analysis (Fig. 2). The antifungal activity of SeNPs against Fusarium spp. is demonstrated through inhibition zone diameters and MIC/MFC values (Table 1). The effectiveness of SeNPs in preserving tomato fruits is shown by comparing decay and mold growth in treated versus untreated fruits (Fig. 3). Microscopic examination reveals structural damage to fungal mycelium after SeNPs treatment (Fig. 4), and the treatment's effect on reducing fungal infection in tomatoes is illustrated (Fig. 5). Cytotoxicity tests indicate low toxicity on normal cells (Fig. 6), and the preparation and treatment of tomatoes at different ripening stages are shown (Fig. 7). The impact of SeNPs on chemical and physical properties of tomatoes is analyzed (Fig. 8 and Fig. 9), and the table highlights the extended shelf-life of SeNPs-treated tomatoes (Table 2).
paper_summary.limitations_noted: The paper does not provide detailed statistical analysis for some of the visual and experimental results, and the long-term effects of SeNPs on human health and the environment are not discussed.
